reviews.review_text:
A good game. The Nemesis system makes it great. Still can't forgive the devs for locking Nemesis behind patents for a decade and then just... sitting on it and doing basically nothing of note with it. 

Still a great game but its legacy has been overshadowed by the titanic, historic fumble of the Nemesis system after this game. 

The game designers deserve accolades, WB and the legal team deserve fates I'm not allowed to describe on Steam.

[h2] Middle-Earth parte 2 (más némesis que nunca) [/h2]

Finalmente después de años pendiente, terminé AMBOS Middle-Earth (preparandome, aunque falte mucho, para el RPG que está desarrollando Warhorse Studios) y puedo confirmar que son juegazos.

Esta segunda entrega mejora todo los aspectos que ya funcionaban muy bien en el primer juego, por lo tanto no tengo nada que reclamar, se siente en algunas cosas un poco viejo, pero la verdad que está espectacular y el que diga lo contrario se la come. Lo disfruté bastante excepto por el DLC del reputo cojido de la gran flauta del moreno (Baranor) que es por tiempo, con puntaje y nose medio raro, lo saque rápido pero tedioso.

Quizá exageraron con los DLC, al menos un poco, el contenido base era bueno y las habilidades nuevas geniales, medio abrumador la cantidad de habilidades y variantes pero te acostumbras y adaptas bien según tu estilo de juego, es un poco repetitivo pero bueh. A la historia, igual que el primer juego, ni pingo de bola, pero más entretenida que el Shadow of Mordor seguramente. Me gustó que el DLC de Eltariel continuara al final del juego base, me agrado la decisión narrativa (boe), pero bueno a quien le importa la historia, aguante el sistema Némesis y matar orcos! 

Los logros la mayoría son solo jugar y jugar, y farmear y farmear y jugar, asique de chill, recomendado para adictos a platinar como yo.

En fin recomendado para toda la familia, tíos, primos, sobrinos, hermanos, pueblerinos, amigos, suegros, y todo aquél descendiente del ♥♥♥♥-sapiens. Les va a gustar tanto como les gusta el pingo.

[b] Recomendado 8.5/10 [/b]

Honestly, this is the most fun I’ve had with a game in a long time. I couldn’t play it when it first came out because my PC couldn’t run it, but I played through the first game countless times back then. This one is even more fun to play.

The story isn’t anything special, but the Nemesis system is amazing, and the gameplay is ridiculously fun. I definitely recommend it.
One of the greatest single player adventure games of all time. The nemesis system is probably the most memorable mechanic in all of games for me and I wish it wasn't stuck under a patent.

Really solid follow-up to the first game. Was glad that they did exactly what I was hoping: expanding upon the nemesis system. 

Some things still kind of annoyed me about the endgame for this one too. They give you super cool armor that you can't upgrade until you basically 100% complete the game, not really sure the point in doing that. I've beaten basically everything I can and I'm about level 50, yet 1/2 the legendary armors require you to be above that to upgrade them. Strange late game design. 

Regardless, still pretty fun.

One of the best games I've played in a long time. I actually played this years ago on a different platform, but picked it up again when I was looking for something LOTR themed. I forgot how good the Nemesis system was. For a game that is a decade old, it still offers better mechanics than many current AAA titles IMO. I was sad to hear that Monolith was shut down and Nemesis is locked behind a patent - but I'm hoping that the new merger will open up a chance for a sequel. 

Overall 5/5. 

P.S. You know it's good when a repeat playthrough 10 years later is worth over 100 hrs.

WB - If you're listening ... please make another. This was so good.
Improves on nearly everything Shadow of Mordor did. More lands, longer story, more enemies, more skills,. Hundreds of hours of fun. Unfortunate we never got a third game.

Played 41.3 hours, started off excited with the gameplay and skill tree. Manipulating orcs was fun. After awhile of grinding, it kinda got reptitive for me. I stopped playing. Thought of unistalling but realised i have the dlc unplayed. Might come back to play this game next time. 3.5/5 rating for me.

Last played this game in 2017 on a MSI GT 740 for my first PC build and at the time the game felt beautiful and quite immersive. 9 years later, playing on a OC 5070 TI and an 360 HZ OLED..the game was truly ahead of its time. It's a shame that the nemesis system hasn't been used more often due to patents and such, but replaying this just for the hell of it and it's still a 9/10. One of the best single players i've ever played
This game is perfect if you love RPGs and the middle earth, it captures the world amazingly.
The story is amazing and the gameplay is satisfying. This is one of the few games I haven't felt like skipping cutscenes for.

A game the no matter how many times I play it, I always come back to. Especially unique for a singleplayer game which should traditionally suffer in replayability, I often find myself rushing through the story just to get to the endgame where I think the game peeks.

I love this game i have 50+ hours. the reason i stopped playing is I got stuck and used mods, My tip is DO NOT use them under any circumstance the game will not be fun anymore if you do. I love the System they have in this game idk why they had to put a patent on it until 2036 so many other games could have adopted it and been much more unique.
nice simple game with great mechanics

WARNING: This review contains spoilers for the story of Shadow of Mordor and Shadow of War

Ok, first off, wow, this is an absolutely AMAZING game. 
For a TLDR, this is my review broken down:
Gameplay: 10/10
Story: 8/10
Level Design: 9/10
Side Quests: 10/10

About a month or two before starting this, I played Shadow of Mordor- this game's predecessor- on Good Old Games and 100%'ed it, and loved Shadow of Mordor, it was amazing and definitely deserved its game of the year nomination in 2014.

This game made Shadow of Mordor look mediocre.
 
Ok, so for those of you who do not know, this game runs off the phenomenal Nemesis System, and let me tell you, this game has hands down the best gameplay and mechanics in any game I've ever played solely because of the originality and fun value of this system. Your enemies can remember you, rank up, cheat death, grow as their own characters and get new powers and abilities (which is really useful for when you mind control them) The siege mechanics are excellent and satisfying and making your captains and war chiefs overpowered beasts capable of tearing down buildings and beheading dragons is so darn awesome. The combat is quick, bloody and powerful. The stealth is (like all mechanics in this game) is intuitive and fun. Leveling up Talion and giving him awesome wraith abilities and making him not even need immortality is really fun too!

The story revolves around Talion, a former ranger of the Black Gate who was killed by the Black Hand of Sauron, but brought back to life thanks to the ghost of Celebrimbor. Talion and Celebrimbor go around Mordor with their new ring of power (which is considered "perfect" because it is free of Sauron's corruption) building their own orc army in a variety of regions around Mordor and taking over fortresses in an attempt to defeat Sauron. This game is set decades before The Lord of the Rings (Approx. 70 years) I won't give anymore spoilers to the story other than its three acts and fourth act Epilogue are phenomenal, and you would be wise to play it.  It also has a variety of collectibles and side quests to produce hours of playtime.

This game is amplified by its many story and gameplay DLC and fun online opportunities (assuming WB doesn't take them down anytime soon due to their money allergy) and this game and it's DLC (and Shadow of Mordor, which I would recommend playing first) go on big sales very often, and you will not waste your time or money by deciding to play them.

Death is truly not the end.

Middle-earth: Shadow of War expands on the first game, but loses steam along the way. It improves the formula with more maps, added mechanics, and a bigger overall scope, which is a welcome upgrade from the original. The gameplay is still solid, and the Nemesis-style systems remain engaging for a while. But repetition sets in hard, and the story just doesn’t hold the same weight this time around. About halfway through, the experience started to feel like a grind, and I lost the motivation to keep going. Even cool ideas, like riding a dragon, don’t quite land, feeling more clunky than exciting. There’s definitely fun to be had here, especially early on, but it doesn’t sustain itself. A decent sequel with good ideas, just not enough staying power.
This game has a very engaging gameplay style, especially the Nemesis system. The gameplay is smooth and very fun with a controller. 10/10.

Such a great game, I have nothing bad to say about it.  Just buy it it's good.  

Nemesis system should be free to use by other teams and the community.  Wasted potential
its a old game but its fun theirs good combat and story i love how some orcs can come back later overall a good game
Lord of the Rings themed Assassin's Creed. Beautiful. What's not to like? I love it.

Shadow of War delivers a highly satisfying gameplay loop. With its perfectly balanced combat and a brilliant, ever-evolving enemy hierarchy, it offers an action-RPG experience that stays fresh from beginning to end.
This is an amazing game, I really suggest to others to play. If you like first middle earth game, you will probably like it too.
this game is lowkey the best fantasy game out there it runs perfectly on high graphics and overall a 10/10 game in my opinion

Very good game. Gameplay is much better than the first game. Story is good.

The combat system is really good after 10-20 hours because you get really sick skills.

Game can be challenging especially towards the end (Defense Missions). Also, quite long game.

Overall 8/10
Finished this back in the day when it came out on PS. Decided to replay again, still plays awesome, story is nice, gameplay is fun, nemesis system is fun. We need more nice LOTR games.
if they just expanded this game it would be a perfect open world. no need to create an entirely new game. this story is perfection.
good game, the nemesis system is actually one of the best game mechanics ever, same other games cant use it
I know it's not cannon, but this game is so much more fun than Shadow of Mordor!

I can't believe I haven't played this game until 2026. What a hackem-up slash-em limitless fun time as a dead elf man. Why didn't Celibrimbor look like this in Rings of Power? Buy it if its on sale, its so addicting.
Fun gameplay, not too complicated.

Completed the game on Xbox and have since made the switch to PC. This was on sale and immediately picked it up again. Amazing LOTR game and the nemesis system makes it just as fun to play a second time. Talion is easily one of my favorite video game characters. If you're a LOTR fan (or really anybody) this is a definite must play.

nice experience playing this especially with orc building and surprisingly it doesn't demand much graphic like most of the triple A games. pretty good fps even without my laptop having a graphic card.

Much better fleshed out than the OG, great gameplay and the Nemesis system is really fun. The orcs have a lot of personality. Writers took a lot of creative freedom with the story and lore (sexy Shelob...), but this is not a story game anyway. I find the difficulty all over the place, including crashes, bugs, messy targeting and confusing upgrades. Brutal difficulty seemed like a sweet spot for me, with keeping the Orcs challenging but not annoyingly tanky, and still making sure I died plenty of times to mess with the Nemesis Orcs. The sieges are also a good addition, felt surprisingly well executed.
Would count this as a classic recommend, especially since we won't soon get similar games thanks to patents

Honest review here

This is a great game, but the uruk captain, warchief, and overlord are too much yapping, wasting time and it's so annoying that we cant skip this ♥♥♥♥, overall it's great! i recommend playing Shadow Of Mordor first

NEEDS a controller. Players who ignore the early story missions for a while will find the combat to be sluggish and very tedious. This is a game that rewards those who keep playing it with new and spectacular ways to assert your authority over Mordor. The weakest story line follows the Gondorians and then Carnan, the spirt god of nature, everything outside of those 2 things, which usually have to do with the Orcs and the established characters (with changes to create a world for Talion to become the Bright Lord), was Intriguing and engaging the whole time. 

I killed an orcs blood brother. dominated him into my ranks after he tried to get revenge, and then I put him in the fight pits over and over again, destroying all the trophies he won for petty cash I didn't need until he died brutally. And you are beneath even that, you vile worm.

This game is almost the same as Shadow of Mordor. However, the first thing you'll notice after loading in is how the camera controls became worse – feeling slippery to the point the camera keeps moving even after the mouse stops. This can be somewhat mitigated by turning down the mouse sensitivity down to the minimum setting, which will make it more comparable to the medium of Shadow of Mordor. If you can get past the slight left-over acceleration.

What you won't be able to get past is that now the orks became even more chatty. Previously, they would stop you mid-charge to tell you how you're about to die. Now, they will stop you mid-charge to tell you their entire life's story. And also how you're about to die. These interruptions in the flow of action cannot be turned off or skipped, making them even more jarring than before. And of course half the time the camera is in the middle of returning to third-person view (and you don't have control of your character yet) you can get hit and even killed by random attacks you had no way of dodging.

Contrary to the reforging system of the previous game, here you can (and have to) swap out your gear for new ones you get from killing orcs. The weapons that were reforged in the first game, have also been returned to their basic, unupgraded form. You might think that this was done to preserve Talion's identity, but the weapons get reforged again by the end of the story. The story that is still as abrupt as it was in the previous game, but now Talion is even more of a pushover.

An other thing that was made worse is the map. In Shadow of Mordor, the map was a very useful blueprint that showed you exactly where what was. Now the map is more of a loose diorama, that only approximately ties into the actual game you're playing. But, you can rotate it! If you were crazy enough to actually bother and throw away any semblance of where North is. The mouse sensitivity on the map is also too high and can't be reduced. The last part that annoys me about the map is the controls. If you want to mark a waypoint – you can't press [right mouse], as that just rotates the map – you have to press [spacebar]. You can't just press [2] to go into the army screen, instead you have to hold [+] for about 5 seconds until the map zooms in and homes in on the keep. Gone is the snappy switching between the screens that was so nice to have in Shadow of Mordor. And of course the menus take ages to fade in from black, slowing down navigation considerably.

The game's persistence has gotten even worse – enemies spawn right in your face or just behind you way more often than they did in the past. You can humiliate a Captain, turn the camera around and he'll spawn right behind you with his entire retinue to be humiliated all over again. I've reliably chained this behavior 5 times in a row. The enemies also continue talking to their friends even after dying to stealth attacks and you don't even get rewarded any XP for killing them in the first place – XP now comes only from killing named characters and doing missions.

The player was also weakened quite a bit. Longbows don't have as much ammo, shortbows can't pierce shields, hammers were added and have an area attack, but it does no damage even to basic grunts. The skill system is the worst offender – you get 3 upgrades to every skill, but they are all side-grades, as you can only have one active at a time. This means you have to choose between dominating an enemy, regaining Elf-shot or replenishing your health fully after a drain. A needlessly horrifying idea as there is absolutely no reason why you couldn't have all of those active at the same time, yet this isn't even the only branching bit of the skill tree that absolutely didn't need to exist.

My last gripe is the bugs, of which there are many. There are invisible bits of geometry you can climb on, but not see. There are visible bits of geometry you go through as though they were invisible. Various objects and characters are much wider than they appear, so you can't pull yourself up a ledge simply because there's a tiny footstool at the top. Drake UI doesn't tell you that you need to hold [shift] to eat an orc you were grabbing. There are missions that soft-lock because the game tells you to release 3 caragors from cages, when the map only contains 2. All characters despawn and stop spawning while you're in a mini-mission to kill/humiliate/dominate an orc, which makes many of the bonus objectives objectively impossible to complete. The game freezes on loading screens in late-game Minas Morgul every 10 minutes. Wrath meter randomly stops filling upon kills. Warchiefs at fortresses respawn almost as fast as you can kill them, turning taking down fortress defenses into a game of whack-a-mole. And finally: many of the orcs in other players' worlds during Online Conquest have every single immunity known to man. Immune to executions, arrows, stealth, fire, frost, poison, curse. Enraged at all times. Use poison weapons that drain health, focus and wrath, kill beasts in 1 hit, immune to vaulting, downs player in one hit due to one epic skill, can take a few hundred hits from the player becaue of an other epic skill, has a shield making them invincible from the front, can rapidly heal to full in the blink of an eye and will defy death once, regaining half their health if you manage to kill them. They call this the 7777 build, apparently. How fun.

Still, I've had more fun with this game that the last one. The player is now much more mobile because sprint can be used at any time, rather than only after acrobatics. You can also use sprint to climb walls faster. You can now chain execute/consume/stealth-kill. You can now summon mounts, minions and bodyguards. Caragors and graugs are now much more mobile and can scale walls. Caragors get the caragath stealth by default and are amusingly powerful at taking down anything not immune to stealth. There are now six maps to play around in, instead of just two. Orks can now be made to fight in fighting pits for levels, saving you a lot of time in leveling them up. You can even mob those 7777 pricks with your army and dominate them for your own use. The DLCs are also not mere afterthoughts, but have their own complete characters with different skill trees and gear upgrades. What the game lacks in polish, it makes up for in variety.

These Game delivers me amazing combat with the smooth Nemesis system making every enemy feel unique and memorable. The game’s large open world, brutal battles, and satisfying character progression make it a very fun action experience.
This has become one of my all-time favorite games. This is the Nemesis system at it's best. Enemies feel personal and each playthrough is unique. I'm currently on my 4th playthrough, first on PC.

I am over 79 hours in this game, and i have completed the main story. Game is great, a big improvement in terms of graphics. Anything else is your usual middle earth experience. You got the iconic nemesis system. We got ratbag and the "suffer me now" meme. Probably one of the most enjoyable RPG i ever played, after Crimson Desert and Witcher 3. Game runs great, no performance issue on my end. I just wish events like rebellions or siege occur more often
Middle-earth: Shadow of War is a gameplay masterpiece. The Nemesis System isn’t just a feature — it’s the soul of the game. It creates stories and enemies you’ll remember forever.

Amazing game, highly recommend.
Essentially Assassin's Creed meets LOTR.

Blade of Galadriel DLC is super easy and very short.  Probably not worth getting unless its on a good deal.
The Baranore DLC was super fun.  Still disappointingly short, but very well done and interesting and enjoyable.

For reference, both the Blade of Galadriel and Baranore DLCs took me ~2.5 hours to 100% where the base game took me over 24 hours to 100%.

Peak sequel to the best LOTR game ever made, doesn't hit quite the same, but hits better in other ways. 

Tons of content, super long. A lot of fun.
there is genuinely not a single thing i dislike about this game. the whole mechanics,system, character design EVERYTHING is perfect

One of those games that quietly becomes an obsession. You start playing for the combat, but stay because random Orc captains somehow create better rivalries and stories than many scripted games. Talion’s ending genuinely hits hard, especially if you’re into the whole Middle-earth lore.
This game is wild! I am so happy to experience this game on the this massive discount. You folks are going to love it!
IF YALL WOULD LET OTHER DEVELOPERS USE THE NEMESIS SYSTEM THIS GAME WOULD BE A 10/10 NOW! Because WB greedy asf, 7/10 only buy it on sale. Eff most triple A dev studios.

Middle-earth: Shadow of War is very fun to play, especially its Nemesis System, which makes the overall gameplay experience much more exciting and engaging. I hope more games in the future use a system like this, or even improve it to feel more alive and immersive. Overall, I’d give it a 9.1/10 — awesome game 👍

Fun but the movement is terrible. the amount of things i get stuck on mid combat or wires i cant properly traverse. so many messed up hit boxes on the map has killed me many times. Just got stuck on a tent and couldn't get off it while fighting a captain, rolled onto it and couldn't get off of it... silly things like this keep happening and its so frustrating.
overall is good, but i have issued every time i start carnan bane the game always crash or freeze
um dos melhores open worlds rpgs da minha vida. jogabilidade gostosa, inimigos desafiadores no late game e sem contar que tem o sistema nemesis, uma das melhores invencoes da humanidade ate agora

I never wrote a review for this game, even though I did enjoy it for a bit, especially the Nemesis System.
Wondering why no other game has a similar system I discovered that WB patented this specific mechanic and isn't doing anything with it but letting it rot, together with this mediocre game. 

So here's my downvote because of it.
it's a fun game. i like it BUT orc traits, armor perks, skill perks and Legendaries just suck to play around.

Not my favorite game.  Seems extremely repetitive. every board is exactly the same. I got stuck on an invincible enemy and that did it. I'm done.  the story isnt great either.  i almost always finish a game but this one no thanks. also, way, way to many button combinations.
even for those who do not give a single living ♥♥♥♥ about the lord of the rings franchise can still love this game to death, I would know since I'm one of em

Bought with 90% discount.
It took 74 hours to complete the 3rd Act.
This has to be one of the best games I've ever p[ayed over the past 40 years.
If you are a LoR fan then this game captures the atmosphere well and does the story justice.

El sistema principal del juego que hace que los orcos tengan rangos distintos, se maten entre ellos para ascender, te traicionen o hagan bandos, es muy divertido y unico. El problema es que es lo unico bueno que tiene el juego para rescatar, el combate es super mediocre, basico y con muy poca profundidad, tiene todo un arbol de habilidades para elegir pero nada aumenta la complejidad del nucleo del juego, sigue siendo re mashear botones, apretar parry o saltar, listo, termino el gameplay loop. La historia es un desastre y pila de veces me arranque a dormir, y el prota lo hacen tan edgie que me genera rechazo. El mundo de por si es horrible, muy poco agradable a la vista, y no es por graficos viejos o nada por el estilo, la direccion artisitica es un desastre, muchas zonas tienen muy poco carisma, y aunque mordor debe ser desolado y mala onda, existen muchos juegos que tienen lugares asi pero esteticamente increibles, como Doom, the witcher o dark souls.
El juego tiene algo divertido seguro, jugar con el sistema de los orcos esta muy bueno e invadir castillos con tu ejercito realmente es un hype. Pero el juego quiere que repitas esas cosas una y otra vez, al punto en que ya no tiene ni gracia.

To be honest, game is awesome so fun so good perfect ...etc
**BUT, BEFORE YOU BUY BE AWARE OF THIS GAME BREAKING, THIS BUG COMPELETLY KILLS YOUR GAME FOREVER**
Just search this "Training Order Bug", this bug didn't get fixed till now and this bug can show up on any of your garrison items and your WORST NIGHTMARE when it shows up on your reassignment order this will forever make you unable to store any of your orcs on your garrison which GG bro your game is done 
There's no fix for this, even if you start a new game nah that won't fix this, so **GG** bro your game got broken forever no way .
Some players said if you avoid opening vendetta chests that gives training orders that might keeps you safe from this bug but WHY THEY DIDN"T FIX IT a game breaking bug like that why it still didn't get fixed till now !!!!

[h1] The best game using Ubisoft formula is a non Ubisoft game & its name is.. [/h1]

[b]Middle Earth: Shadow of War[/b] is easily one of my most favorite games of all time to date & while the nemesis system is quite impressive, in my honest opinion, its not the only attractive feature within this gem when we inspect it closely. 
[b]This is one of those games that feel like a work of art & its a cult classic that I personally believe should be case studied when it comes to the modern gaming scene.[/b]

Kindly allow me to elaborate by discussing story, designs & characters first before writing about the amazing combat & systems.

[h2]Regarding story, designs & character building:[/h2]

- This is a game that's easy to resonate with, at least in my experience. Melancholic music, a so very human story, the heartwarming depictions of comradery, the few yet very memorable NPCs aside from orcs.

- While there are a few plot holes in this game, I think its very well written as there's lots of sentience within this game. Its not a war against orcs, its a war against ego & corruption even that of the self. [spoiler]The rings are a metaphor of power & dominion & its corruptive effects on mortals.[/spoiler] For example, each one of the Nazgul's background is a different story on one of [spoiler]evil's ways into a mortal's heart.[/spoiler] I think its a good extension to Tolkien's messages in the original LOTR works.

- I loved the dynamic between Talion & Celebrimbor. I liked both characters. I liked how poetic Celebrimbor was but also the subtle signs of how his warm pride [spoiler]was turning to cold ego.[/spoiler] I loved the poems they collected together as well as those artifacts & their different commentary & opinions about each. I loved how the game didn't need to focus on romance to design a good relation dynamic. The game shows you can make good relations in videogames & media fully platonic, a bond forged in hardship & comradery between people of varied thoughts & worlds. The build up was great, it made the well foreseen plot twist hit harder not because of surprise but because, you see how beautiful their [spoiler]comradery was. Talion didn't seem even mad at Celebrimbor but mourned for a comrade fallen to corruption which is shown in one of Talion's quotes later where he expresses howmuch he misses Celebrimbor's company. The same fate Talion realized could also be his one day.[/spoiler]

- I liked Shelob & [spoiler]the duality between her & Sauron & how aligned they were in some core similarities despite their contradictions.[/spoiler] 
I liked her quotes that gave the game more philosiphical depth. The game took the ravenous giant spider & turned her into a more poetic sentient. Even her seductive human form was in service to her character's narrative in this game just like [spoiler]Sauron's fair handsome form. They're both masks & Shelob mentions it later.[/spoiler]

- I liked the design of Idril, the game's representation of youth who was neither ugly nor sexualized showing you don't have to deliberately make a female character ugly so she won't seem sexualized. A youthful brave shieldmaiden princess in full armor so humanely vulnerable but also steadfast & courageous not weak. It showed you only need to treat the female character as a dignified human design, her looking natural feminine was never a problem nor a sexualization.

- I liked Baranor, the game's personification of loyalty & integrity. A black character that's given equal importance or close to equal, not above nor below other characters & even some MCs. A sign of a cohesive & balanced design framework.

- I liked Eltariel, the elf. An elf yet acting so human, full of mistakes yet blind to them & [spoiler]ultimately that was her downfall but also her wake.[/spoiler]

- There are more than a few smart & foretelling quotes in the game that add to the narrative's depth like that one Eltariel says: [spoiler]In the war against darkness, sometimes stalemate is victory.[/spoiler]

- Also Bruz, Ratbag, Ranger, Zog presented an interesting dynamic. They were reflective of the [spoiler]MCs' descent into the very thing they were fighting.[/spoiler]

- Carnan & how awesome her overall design is in this game & the sweet subtle details like the difference between Talion's & Celebrimbor's mannerisms towards her as well as Shelob.

- Also, the fact the game makes you contextually feel corrupt/messed up when you dominate & humiliate orcs & make them deranged & so on. The game makes you decide these acts yourself as the player. This is further emphasized by certain arcs like Bruz's. It really puts you in the MC's shoes. This is one of the few games that does that, the only other modern age game that does that is Vampyr but in a different way through its feeding/level up mechanism.

[b]Now, that I'm done gushing about the lore & character designs & development within the game, I can get to reviewing the amazing & mostly unique combat related systems in this game.[/b]

[h2]Regarding combat:[/h2]

- The enemies & the orcs & how goofy & variable they are thanks to the Nemesis system & the roguish & haunting/ghostly, necromantic vibes & the Arkham Assassin Creed hybrid sort of combat.
All these are amazing when we talk about the combat as well as the late game armor sets that provide some build variety.

- The variety of interaction options with the environment & the fast paced stealth & amazing traversal mechanics with the distinctive ghostly visual & sound effects make for such a unique vibe specially if you're into undead & necromancers. 
It also has some tactical depth, we're not talking Thief series nor Dishonored nor Splinter Cell Chaos Theory level of tactical depth but in comparison to most of hybrid stealth games that use the Ubisoft formula like the Assassins Creed series, Shadow of War is very tactical in a satisfying power fantasy stealth sort of way. 
The nemesis system setting random weaknesses for orcs plays a good role in this tactical depth as well since some orcs have phobia or weakness to say fire or the flies & this can help you decide on what sort of tactics & traps to set up for different encounters against different orcs. 

- Then there are the creatures you can tame & mount including even dragons which you can fly on & have them roast the enemies with their fiery breath. There is so much variety when it comes to combat & interactivity in this game in its own interesting way. 

- Of course let's not forget the indirect online content be it orc duels, missions where you avenge other players against the orc that killed them & conquering other players' forts & getting to fight their orcs head on.
All this is amazing eventhough hackers do mess the online fort content sometimes by using cheat engine on the orcs protecting their fort to give them crazy broken meme stats.

- Admittedly, there is a somewhat unfair difficulty spike on the game's second arc. That's also where things start to feel more grindy when kind of breaks the flow, in my opinion.

- The best part when it comes to combat & management aspects in this game is howmuch it feels like an indepth & action packed Pokemon game where the pokemons are orcs who have more variety & feel way more personal with their own randomly generated drama & shenanigans again thanks to the unfortunately patented Nemesis system.
The closest I've seen to the Nemesis system was the mercenaries system in AC Odyssey but unfortunately, while it was a nice effort, it only achieved a fragment of how fun & more indepth the Nemesis system is.

[h2]Regarding Pricing & Net Value:[/h2]

The game's definitive edition on the 90% off sale is quite the steal & that edition has all DLCs. Many of the DLCs provide lots of content as well.

 [h2]Overall Rating:[/h2]

Middle Earth: Shadow of War is borderline gamified literature in the modern age of gaming if you ask me & I love it to bits. Its one of the very rare games that get a no brainer [b]10/10[/b] from me.
Fun and dynamic system.... but Mediocre (+woke) story & weird and awkward main missions. Ending and end contents were extra underwhelming.

However there's one feature that is annoying in both games but it's way worse here,  that is unskippable captain speeches. What is worse, in Shadow of Mordor speeches seemed to be shorter and there were less captain encounters overall. It is so bad that in fact I don't think I'll finish the game. Over all I think Shadow of Mordor was fun, but you can skip Shadow of War entirely.

Awful game, but its fun to play.

Sure it is slightly better than the first game, emphasis on slightly, however there are still braindead developer decisions all over the place, like the blue Sauron eyes saturating the map (are we 5yo?), the fact Gollum once again has a core presence (again, are we 5yo?), Shelob, one of the divine and oldest entities of middle earth is suddenly a pretty female human (instead of her menacing arachnid form in LOTR). Time limits on various quests, what is this? Are we still in the 1990's mindset of game concepts?. Orc's talk WAY TOO MUCH, again are we 5yo? since when are Orcs cute, funny, friendly, and everybodies friend.

Combat is less fun and more irritating, frustrating and unbalanced, counters fail 70% of the time even if you timed it right, out of every 10 vendetta missions, 7 fail due to some "scripted npc insta kill event" that LITERALLY happens out of nowhere. The loot system is insufferable, with wrong/misleading colour formatting, legendaries are locked behind "ONLINE MODE challenges" (because thats what we want in a LOTR game - copying Ubisoft much?) legit i have held 9 pieces of legendary items, 7 slotted, 60 hours later AND THEY ARE STILL NOT UPGRADED - why? because upgrading your low level legendary items is more tedious in this game than ESO or BDO for some bizarre reason. I just end up 'destroying' every single piece of "gear" I find, because why bother, its all trash anyway and legendaries are clearly the intended options.

Movement controls, are still some of the WORST in gaming, if you want to run in a straight line, Thalion instead decides to fly up a wall in the opposite direction, if you try to move forward he instead gets stuck when a small 1cm high pebble is in the way. 

I dont know how people are giving this nonsense high reviews, in 2026 it is quite an awful experience and to be honest, the developer making their own adjustments to Tolkien lore, to me is disgraceful, like why would you? There is literally nothing that needs changing within Tolkien lore, it is just an insult if anything.

5/10

I recommend this only if one is looking for a LoTR themed game that has little to do with the actual lore. 

However, while the game can be fun, the Nemesis system gets exhausting really fast. Unskippable orc speeches got me rolling my eyes and the lack of variety in quests bogged this game down to such a degree that the grind lost all the fun. Set a mission, go kill someone, rinse and repeat. 

Past Act III there's literally nothing else to do but grind endlessly and it's just boring all over. Not to mention that the mount system is unyielding and they are far too exhausting to control to really serve the player in any way. I can say I liked the first game much more. It's really disappointing that Act III, that could've been an entire game on its own if given a plotline, has not been either explored or made interesting in any way despite having a wonderful set up for an incredible story if done right.

As an ending note - I will be happy to never encounter the Nemesis system ever again.
little repetitive and very little story line but I enjoy playing occassionally

Middle-earth: Shadow of War — одна з найцікавіших ігор у своєму жанрі

На мою думку, Middle-earth: Shadow of War — це надзвичайно цікава та захоплива гра, яка заслуговує на увагу кожного шанувальника пригодницьких екшенів.

Окремо хочеться відзначити систему Немезиди та взаємодію з орками. Полювання на капітанів реалізоване дуже вдало: доводиться вишукувати потрібних супротивників, вивчати їхні сильні та слабкі сторони, перемагати, підкорювати або знищувати. Кожен капітан має власну зовнішність, характер, манеру поведінки та ефектну презентацію, завдяки чому вони справді запам'ятовуються. Саме ця система робить гру унікальною та постійно створює нові цікаві ситуації.

Ще одним величезним плюсом для мене став рівень складності «Брутальний». Рідко зустрічаються ігри, де підвищена складність реалізована настільки вдало. Головний герой завдає більше шкоди, швидше накопичує Міць і Лють, а сильних капітанів можна знищувати за лічені секунди, якщо правильно використовувати їхні слабкості. Водночас вороги стають набагато небезпечнішими: одна пропущена контратака або невдале оточення можуть швидко призвести до поразки.

На цьому рівні складності використання стелсу, особливостей оточення та вразливостей орків до вогню, отрути чи інших факторів стає не просто корисним, а необхідним. На відміну від багатьох інших ігор, де висока складність означає лише збільшене здоров'я та шкоду ворогів, тут вона змушує думати, планувати та використовувати всі механіки гри.

Саме тому я рекомендую проходити гру на рівні складності «Брутальний», адже саме на ньому вона розкривається найкраще.

Сюжет і загальна історія також залишили приємне враження. Світ гри виглядає атмосферно, а персонажі добре запам'ятовуються. Особливо це стосується орків: іноді навіть шкода втрачати приручених воїнів, до яких встигаєш прив'язатися. Водночас гра завжди дозволяє знайти ще кращих бійців або самостійно виростити могутнього воїна — від звичайного рядового орка до легендарного капітана.

У підсумку можу сказати, що Middle-earth: Shadow of War — це чудова гра з унікальними механіками, захопливим геймплеєм і великою кількістю можливостей. Однозначно рекомендую до проходження.

Shadow of Mordor was a real nice game, but this sequel completely went the Ubisoft collectibles route while trying to literally be as annoying as humanly possible.

The combat loop is infuriating. The game makes absolutely sure that you are always—and will always be—completely outnumbered, yet it gives you very bad crowd control and barely any effective AOE damage to deal with the chaos. Instead of feeling like a shadow in Mordor you have this weird strategy armee kind of sideplay that really doesnt pull off to well (Nemesis).

Even the mechanics seem designed specifically to annoy you. Flying on a dragon should be a highlight, but it ends up being the most clunky ♥♥♥♥ ever put into a game. The story isn't even worth mentioning, and to top it all off, everything is designed to be incredibly grindy and repetitive. I mean at this point i am not even sure if i finsihed the story as it doesnt even have a proper outline that shows you. Instead it seems like it has 3-4 different Storys at the same time so i finished all but one i think? That is a massive flaw in a game that is inherently just not fun to play.

They took a great foundation and ruined it with really obviously tedious systems.

So I bought this game when it first came out and thought it was terrible, but now it's fine and pretty fun. (Ignore my playtime, I've had it on in the background while doing other things.)

Shadow of War is a phenomenal sequel to a nearly perfect LOTR game and one of the pinnacle sequel games available. Having previously played Shadow of Mordor I can say that both have a unique quality to them that most modern games do not have. I am referring to their complex nemesis system. Having the enemies remember the player through encounters to add a layer of personality to them as well as strengthen custom narratives and storylines. Creating a compelling character such as Talion was also a great choice. Furthermore, you get to experience his evolution from a grieving father and husband in the previous game to conquering middle earth was a the best part of the gaming experience to me. adding more to the combat that was not previously there in Shadow of Mordor was a great addition to the game. However, despite the amount of improvement in both the game mechanics and gameplay I have two drawbacks to this experience. Firstly how distinguished enemies are to each other. I feel that the first game had designed the orcs more effectively. You could see the more intricate details and design in the older models compared to shadow of war. There was more customization available while the current game has a more serialized pre-established base models that can be generated. After a certain point in time the amount of orcs that you encounter seem more bland as you become accustomed to their appearances. Secondly. their DLC added little substance to the original game. Two additional tribes sounded fine on paper but in reality their limited enemy variety issues still persisted. The two campaigns added were quite short for my liking as it felt as though it only  took just about half an hour each to complete their main storylines. They felt more like the side quests in the main campaign than them being DLC worthy additions to the game. As a result and careful consideration of the facts presented and my own experiences with the game, I can place Shadow of War at an 8.0/10 with assurance.

I'm only ten hours into playing Middle-earth: Shadow of War and I'm completely captivated. It is a really good game. I've got to say, the Nemesis System is simply amazing, with each orc seeming to be its own individual, complete with its own grievances, personality traits, and even backstories. The intrigue that develops in this game is genuinely intriguing. The fighting itself is fluid and intense; the fortress conquests are impressive; the exploration is immersive; and the storyline is engrossing. Overall, if you enjoy action adventure games that are also deep and personal, I can highly recommend this one.
